name: Bead
description:
Library component. Models one row in the beads database. Status is a typed integer: 0=Open, 1=Hooked, 2=Closed (mirrors internal/beads/status.go's enum). Each `gt sling` invocation creates a new bead row and transitions it from status=0 to status=1 via hookBeadWithRetry (sling.go:918).

v0.4 (defense-doc P2.5): adds a `formula` typed-int variable, initialized per-instance via the 148-shape `formulaId` option. Lets the project model multiple beads of multiple formulas on the same assignee. The HookedSingleton invariant becomes formula-keyed: `Bead_i.status = 1 AND Bead_j.status = 1 AND Bead_i.formula = Bead_j.formula` is the violation pattern. With one cross-formula bead in the project, two beads of different formulas can both be Hooked without violating, while two beads of the same formula cannot.

v0.3 (defense-doc P2.4): per-instance initial status is parameterized via the 148-shape `initial` option, letting the same component model 'a fresh bead created right now' (initial=0) and 'a bead that was already hooked at t=0 from a prior sling' (initial=1) without two component variants. The multi-cycle redesign no longer uses the prior shorthand, but the option remains for other models.

The Close path (status 1 -> 2 via gt done / bd.Close) is NOT modeled in this v0.4 -- the #3768 bug demo doesn't need it. Add a CLOSE in-port + transition when modeling the proposed fix that pre-closes existing hooked beads.

HOOK.description: receives a hook event when a sling targets this bead. Tagged 'Sling'. Real: sling.go:918 hookBeadWithRetry. Triple guard `status = 0` enforces the lifecycle: a Hooked bead receiving Sling does nothing (the bug is that sling.go creates a NEW row each time, not that it overwrites an existing one's status).

actors.name: Bead
actors.owner: Single bead's status as a typed integer (0=Open, 1=Hooked, 2=Closed) plus a formula identifier. Both initialized from per-instance options.

triples.event: sling targets this bead: status 0 -> 1 (open -> hooked)
